title: P300 dataset BI2014a from a "Brain Invaders" experiment
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalographic (EEG) recordings from 71 healthy subjects performing a visual P300-based brain-computer interface task using the Brain Invaders videogame. Participants engaged with an oddball paradigm involving pseudo-random flashes of 36 symbols on a grid to elicit P300 event-related potentials. The study evaluated the feasibility of a calibration-less P300 BCI system using 16 dry electrodes, with real-time adaptive classification for online feedback during gameplay. The experiment was designed as a screening session for potential candidates for a broader multi-user BCI study.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using 16 active dry gold-alloy electrodes (g.Sahara cap, g.tec) at 512 Hz sampling rate with right earlobe reference and FZ ground. The visual oddball paradigm presented 36 symbols (1 target, 35 non-targets) in 12 groups with pseudo-random flashing. Each session comprised 9 levels with up to 8 attempts per level to identify the target symbol. A real-time adaptive Riemannian Minimum Distance to Mean (RMDM) classifier provided online feedback. Hardware synchronization was achieved using a USB digital-to-analog converter without digital filtering during acquisition. Up to three game sessions were conducted per subject.
